name: Stoneback Warehouse
description: A long, low stone building at the end of the docks, built where the river road meets the wharf. It stores goods in transit — grain moving downriver, ironwork moving upriver, and everything merchants need held between market days. The building is unremarkable by design. The legitimate operation is managed by Fren of Coldwater Vale, who keeps delivery schedules with the earnest confusion of a man who still doesn't understand commerce but has memorized every route by repetition. The loading dock faces the river. The back door faces Blue Lantern Alley, which is less coincidental than it appears. Kael Stormrunner's river barge ties up at the warehouse dock more often than a legitimate smuggler should feel comfortable with, but Old Mattock never asks questions and Fren never notices.

sounds: Crates being dragged across stone floors
Fren reading delivery manifests aloud to himself
The slap of river water against the dock pilings
Kael's barge creaking at its moorings
Pax grunting as he lifts things no one else can
The back door opening and closing at odd hours
smells: Burlap, grain dust, and hemp rope
River damp — the stones never fully dry
Lamp oil from the dock lanterns
Whatever Kael's barge is carrying this week
atmosphere.lighting: High narrow windows and the open loading doors during the day. Oil lamps at the dock after dark. The back corners stay dim.

layout.name: Main Storage Floor
layout.description: Organized by merchant and destination. Crates, barrels, and sacks in rows. Fren's system is labeled with a code only he understands — which works because only he needs to.

layout.name: The Loading Dock
layout.description: River-facing platform where barges tie up and goods are moved in or out. A crane for heavy loads that Phineas built and that works approximately 80% of the time.

layout.name: Fren's Office
layout.description: A desk near the front door with delivery schedules, route maps, and a jar of cookies Oma sends over. The maps have Fren's hand-drawn annotations that are surprisingly accurate.

layout.name: The Back Corridor
layout.description: Connects to the alley side. Officially for emergency egress. Unofficially, for goods that arrive without paperwork and leave without being inventoried.

hooks: A shipment marked 'ceramic tiles' arrived last week that no merchant has claimed — and it's heavier than ceramic should be
Fren's delivery manifests don't match the actual inventory count, but he can't figure out why and is getting anxious
Kael has been making more trips upstream than usual and paying Fren extra to not mention it
Something in the back corner has been making noise at night — Fren refuses to investigate alone
Old Mattock saw someone on the dock at midnight who shouldn't have been there and is deciding whether to tell the captain

worldContext.defaultTone: practical with an undercurrent of secrets
